title: Retirement
description: Practice understanding of retirement and retirement expenses.

assessment_items.question_content:
Retirement is a time in life when people stop working and need to have enough money saved to cover their expenses.

**Which of these is a main retirement expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: long term care
saving for grandchildren's education
going on a seniors-only cruise

assessment_items.question_content:
Retirement is a time in life when people stop working and need to have enough money saved to cover their expenses.

**Which of these is a main retirement expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying for monthly living costs
saving for grandchildren's education
playing bingo with friends

assessment_items.question_content:
Retirement is a time in life when people stop working and need to have enough money saved to cover their expenses.

**Which of these is a main retirement expense?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying for healthcare costs
traveling to visit grandchildren
volunteering at the local community center

assessment_items.question_content:
Housing is one of the major retirement expenses and it includes mortgage, or rent, payments, taxes, utilities, maintenance and insurance.

**Which of the following situations would help *decrease* your housing expenses in retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: paying off your mortgage before you retire
moving to a larger house so kids and grandkids can stay over
adding an extra bedroom downstairs for easy access

assessment_items.question_content:
Transportation is one of the major expenses during retirement, and it includes costs related to owning and maintaining a vehicle, public transportation, and other travel expenses.


**Which of the following situations would help *decrease* your transportation expenses in retirement?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: using public transportation more frequently
buying a new car so that you don't have to ride the bus
taking more vacations that require air travel

assessment_items.question_content:
Food is an important expense during retirement, and it includes grocery shopping, dining out, and other food-related costs.

**Which of the following situations would help *decrease* your food expenses in retirement?**



[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: shopping for groceries in bulk
eating out at restaurants so you don’t have to buy any groceries
having food delivered so you don’t have to pay for gas

assessment_items.question_content:
When planning for retirement, it is important to be aware of future challenges.

**How does [[☃ definition 1]] affect the amount you need to save for retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.definition: the average number of years a person can expect to live

choices.content: You may need to save more money to cover costs for a longer period of time.
You may need to save less money because you will receive Social Security for longer period of time.
You won't need to save any more or less money because life expectancy only affects life insurance.

assessment_items.question_content:
When planning for retirement, it is important to be aware of future challenges.

**How can inflation affect your retirement savings?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Inflation can *decrease* the value of your savings over time.
Inflation can *increase* the value of your savings over time.
Inflation only affects the value of your savings if you invest in stocks.

assessment_items.question_content:
Government programs, like Social Security, can be an important source of income during retirement.

**How can the instability of government programs affect your retirement planning?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The instability of government programs may cause you to rely more on personal savings for retirement.
The instability of government programs can make it easier to plan for retirement.
The instability of government programs does not affect retirement planning.

assessment_items.question_content:
Saving money for retirement is important, but there are other strategies you can use to increase your retirement income. 

**Which of the following strategies can help you increase your income during retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: retire as early as possible
spend all your savings before applying for Social Security
delay your retirement by a few years

assessment_items.question_content:
It is essential to save money for when you stop working, but there are other ways you can boost the amount of money you have during your retirement years.

**What strategy can you use to increase the amount of money you have during retirement?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: retire as early as possible
invest your Social security payments in high-risk stocks to maximize your returns
downsize to a smaller home to decrease your expenses

assessment_items.question_content:
Saving money for retirement is important, but there are other strategies you can use to increase your retirement income. 

**Which of the following strategies can help you increase your income in retirement?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: retire as early as possible
moving to warmer climate to improve your quality of life
working part-time
